Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows poor glycemic control (elevated HbA1c and fasting glucose), dyslipidemia (high total cholesterol, LDL, and triglycerides), and mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Renal function and electrolytes are within normal limits, and there is no evidence of microalbuminuria. Recommend optimization of diabetes and lipid management, and further evaluation of acid-base status if clinically indicated.
